question_text: A knife cuts better than a spoon because it has a sharper edge. Which of these best explains why?

options.label: A
options.text: The knife is made of stronger material

options.label: B
options.text: A sharp edge concentrates the force over a smaller area, creating higher pressure

options.label: C
options.text: The knife weighs more than a spoon

options.label: D
options.text: A sharp edge reduces the weight of the tool

explanation: Since pressure = force/area, a sharp knife edge has a very small contact area. The same pushing force concentrated on this tiny area creates much higher pressure, allowing it to cut through food easily.
